nameMeanings.name: FitzHugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Rippingale
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Rippingale (Lincs).

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Canadian Hénault or directly of French Hunault (see Henault 2).
Catalan (Añó): Castilianized form of Anyó, probably from the old personal name Annone.
Japanese: variant of   Anno  .

variants.name: Anno
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masayuki, Tadashi, Yoshito.
Japanese: written 安野 ‘peaceful plain’. This surname is rare in Japan proper, and may have originated in the Ryūkyū Islands, where it is pronounced   Ano  .

variants.name: Henault
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Lucien, Martial, Pierre, Camille, Fernand, Laurier, Monique, Normand, Philippe.
French (Hénault): from the ancient Germanic personal name Haginwald, composed of the elements hagin ‘enclosure, protected place’ and wald ‘power, authority’.
Altered form (Hénault) of French Hunault: from the ancient Germanic personal name Hunwald, composed of the elements hun ‘bear cub’ and wald ‘power, authority’. Compare Ano and Eno .

nameMeanings.name: Kirkby
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Kirby  .

variants.name: Kirby
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in northern and eastern England called Kirby or Kirkby, from Old Norse kirkja ‘church’ + bȳ ‘farmstead, village’.
Irish: adopted for Ó Garmhaic ‘descendant of Cíarmhac’, a personal name meaning ‘dark son’. Compare Kerwick .
Irish: Anglicized form of Mac Geirble ‘son of Geirble’, a personal name of uncertain origin. The name is preserved in the townland name of Carrowkeribly in County Mayo.

nameMeanings.name: Underwood
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived below a wood on a hillside, from Middle English under + wude, wode ‘wood’, or a habitational name for someone from any place so named.

History: Joseph Underwood emigrated from England to Watertown, MA, in 1637. William Underwood came from England to Concord, MA, before 1640, later settling in Chelmsford, MA. In VA, the name was established by Thomas William Underwood, who came from Norfolk, England, in 1675.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood was born in 1195, in Rippingale, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Hugh FitzJohn, was 27 and his mother, Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, was 25. He had at least 1 son with Ivette.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood est né en 1195, à Rippingale, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Hugh FitzJohn, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Ivette.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood 1195 in Rippingale, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hugh FitzJohn 27 und seine Mutter Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn 25. Er hatte mit Ivette mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood è nato il nel 1195 nel Rippingale, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Hugh FitzJohn, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Ivette.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood(이)가 1195년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 리핀갤에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hugh FitzJohn(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Ivette(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood nasceu em 1195, em Rippingale, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Hugh FitzJohn, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Ivette.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood родился в 1195 г. в местности риппингеил, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Hugh FitzJohn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, 25. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Ivette.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John FitzHugh I of Rippingale and Kirkby Underwood nació en 1195, en Rippingale, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Hugh FitzJohn, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Mrs. Hugh FitzJohn, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Ivette.
